info.title: Ludo.ai API
info.description:
The Ludo.ai API enables you to generate game assets programmatically, including images, videos, 3D models, spritesheets, and audio.

# Getting Started

## Base URL
All API requests should be made to: `https://api.ludo.ai/api`

## Authentication
Include your API key in the `Authorization` header:
```
Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY
```

## Timeout
Asset generations may take some time, so make sure you include a generous timeout for your requests (at least 180 minutes)

## Async Usage
You can use `request_id` to build fire-and-forget workflows. Tag any generation request with a `request_id`, then poll the corresponding results endpoint to pick up the output when it's ready — no need to keep the connection open.

1. Pass an optional `request_id` string in your generation request body
2. Poll the corresponding results endpoint (e.g. `GET /assets/images/results?request_id=YOUR_ID`) to retrieve the output

Each asset type has a results endpoint:
- `GET /assets/images/results` — images
- `GET /assets/sprites/results` — spritesheets
- `GET /assets/videos/results` — videos
- `GET /assets/audio/results` — audio
- `GET /assets/3d-models/results` — 3D models

Results are available for 7 days and return the 100 most recent API-generated assets. The `request_id` query parameter is optional — omit it to list all recent results.

## Asset URL Expiration
All generated asset URLs (images, spritesheets, videos, audio, 3D models) are temporary and expire after **7 days**. If you need to keep an asset, download it before it expires.

## Request Format
- Use `Content-Type: application/json` for all requests
- All responses are returned in JSON format

## Credits
Each API call consumes credits from your account. The credit cost varies by endpoint and is documented in each endpoint's description.

> **Note:** All API requests consume credits, even if your subscription includes unlimited credits for the web application. Check your account dashboard for your current API credit balance.

ApiKey.name: Authorization
ApiKey.description:
For accessing the API a valid API Key token must be passed in all the queries in
the 'Authorization' header.
The following syntax must be used in the 'Authorization' header: ApiKey xxxxxx.yyyyyyy.zzzzzz

get.description: Validates an API key. Returns 200 if valid, 403 if invalid.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "https://api.ludo.ai/api/auth/validate-api-key" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/image" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"image_type":"generic","prompt":"string"}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result
post.description:
Generates an image from a text prompt

> **Credits:** This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/image/style" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"style_image":"<url> OR data:image/png;base64,...","prompt":"string","image_type":"generic"}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result
post.description:
Generates a new image based on a text prompt while maintaining the visual style of a reference image. Use this to create new characters, objects, or scenes that match an existing art style.

> **Credits:** This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/image/edit" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"image":"<url> OR data:image/png;base64,...","prompt":"string"}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result
post.description:
Edits an existing image based on a text prompt describing the desired changes. Use this for modifications like 'remove the background', 'add clouds to the sky', 'make it darker', 'change colors', etc.

> **Credits:** This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/sprite/animate" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"motion_prompt":"string","initial_image":"<url> OR data:image/png;base64,..."}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint's credit cost varies by model and duration. Available models: Blitz (1.9 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1.2s = 4, 1.5s = 4, 2s = 4, 2.5s = 4.8, 3s = 5.7, 3.5s = 6.7, 4s = 7.6) · Eagle (2.6 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 2s = 5.2, 3s = 7.8, 4s = 10.4) · Eagle with Audio (3.1 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 2s = 6.2, 3s = 9.3, 4s = 12.4) · Chaos (1.4 credits/s, min 4 credits; 4s = 5.6)
post.description:
Animates a sprite image based on a provided text prompt

> **Credits:** This endpoint's credit cost varies by model and duration. Available models: Blitz (1.9 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1.2s = 4, 1.5s = 4, 2s = 4, 2.5s = 4.8, 3s = 5.7, 3.5s = 6.7, 4s = 7.6) · Eagle (2.6 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 2s = 5.2, 3s = 7.8, 4s = 10.4) · Eagle with Audio (3.1 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 2s = 6.2, 3s = 9.3, 4s = 12.4) · Chaos (1.4 credits/s, min 4 credits; 4s = 5.6).

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/sprite/pose" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"image":"<url> OR data:image/png;base64,...","pose":"Idle (Front)"}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result
post.description:
Generates a new pose for an existing sprite image. Only works with sprite image types (not icons, screenshots, etc.). IMPORTANT: Use this endpoint BEFORE calling /assets/sprite/animate to get the best animation results. The starting pose should match your intended animation - for a running animation, generate a running pose first; for an idle-to-attack animation, generate an idle pose; for a jumping animation, generate a jump preparation pose, etc.

> **Credits:** This endpoint consumes 0.5 credits per result.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.ludo.ai/api/assets/sprite/transfer-motion" \
  -H "Authorization: ApiKey YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"image":"data:image/png;base64,..."}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python

post.x-credit-description: This endpoint's credit cost varies by model and duration. Available models: Tango (4 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 1.5s = 6, 2s = 8, 2.5s = 10, 3s = 12, 3.5s = 14, 4s = 16)
post.description:
Transfers motion from a video onto a static sprite image, producing an animated spritesheet

> **Credits:** This endpoint's credit cost varies by model and duration. Available models: Tango (4 credits/s, min 4 credits; 1s = 4, 1.5s = 6, 2s = 8, 2.5s = 10, 3s = 12, 3.5s = 14, 4s = 16).

get.description: Lists available animation presets for use with the transferMotion endpoint

GenerateImagePayload.description: Payload for generating an image from text prompt

image_type.description: What kind of image to generate.

prompt.description: Text description of the image to generate. The more detailed the prompt, the more accurate the image will be.

art_style.description: Visual art style for the sprite (e.g., "Pixel Art", "Cartoonish", "Realistic").

perspective.description: Camera angle/view for the sprite (e.g., "Side view", "Front view", "Isometric").

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the output image.

n.description: Number of image variations to generate

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateWithStylePayload.description: Payload for generating new content while maintaining the visual style of a reference image

style_image.description: URL or base64-encoded reference image whose visual style should be matched.

prompt.description: Text description of what to generate (e.g., "a warrior character", "a forest background", "a treasure chest").

image_type.description: What kind of image to generate.

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

EditImagePayload.description: Payload for editing an existing image based on text instructions

image.description: URL or base64-encoded source image to edit.

prompt.description: Text description of the changes to make (e.g., "remove the background", "add clouds to the sky", "make it darker", "change the color scheme to warmer tones").

reference_image.description: Optional URL or base64-encoded reference image for style or content guidance.

n.description: Number of edited variations to generate (1-4).

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

AnimateSpritePayload.description: Payload for generating an animated spritesheet from a static image. Input images can either be provided in base64 or URL. If the image was generated using Ludo, ideally it should be generated using the "sprite", "sprite-vfx" or "ui_asset" type.

motion_prompt.description: Text description of the desired animation (e.g., "walking", "jumping", "idle breathing", "attack slash").

initial_image.description: The url OR base64 of the starting frame image to animate. This is the base sprite that will be brought to life.

final_image.description: The url OR base64 of ending frame image. When provided, the animation will interpolate between the initial and final frames.

loop.description: Trim the animation at the beginning or end to create a seamless loop. Not guaranteed to produce a perfect loop.

crop.description: Crop sprite frames to fit content. Results in smaller spritesheets but inconsistent frame sizes across different animations.

frames.description: Number of frames in the output spritesheet.

frame_size.description: Size of each frame in pixels (width and height). 0 is for maximum resolution. -1 is for AI 1.5x upscaling. -9 is for matching the size and position of the input frame.

margin_ratio.description: Amount of padding around the sprite as a ratio (0.0 to 1.0). Only used when margin_ratio_mode is "manual". Higher values provide more space for expansive animations.

margin_ratio_mode.description: Controls how margins are applied around the sprite.

image_type.description: Type of sprite being animated. Affects generation parameters and styling.

model.description:
Model to use. Available models:
- "blitz" (Blitz): Visual Quality: 4/10 · Motion Stability: 8/10 · 1.9 credits/s, min 4
- "eagle" (Eagle): Visual Quality: 8/10 · Motion Stability: 5/10 · 2.6 credits/s, min 4
- "eagle-audio" (Eagle with Audio): Visual Quality: 8/10 · Motion Stability: 5/10 · 3.1 credits/s, min 4
- "chaos" (Chaos): Visual Quality: 6/10 · Motion Stability: 3/10 · 1.4 credits/s, min 4
Legacy aliases: "standard" → blitz, "new" → chaos.

duration.description:
Duration in seconds. Available values depend on the model:
- blitz: 1.2, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5, 4
- eagle: 1, 2, 3, 4
- eagle-audio: 1, 2, 3, 4
- chaos: 4

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

gif.description: When true, generates an animated GIF from the spritesheet and returns it in gif_url. Disabled by default to reduce response time.

individual_frames.description: When true, extracts each frame from the spritesheet as an individual image and returns the URLs in individual_frame_urls.

spritesheet_with_background.description: When true, also returns the spritesheet with background intact (before background removal). Useful for manually fixing background removal issues. The with-background spritesheet URL will be in spritesheet_with_background_url.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GeneratePosePayload.description: Payload for generating a new pose for an existing sprite

image.description: URL or base64-encoded source sprite image to generate a new pose from.

pose.description: Target pose for the sprite. Use the value "Other" to generate other poses not listed in the accepted values, and fill the field description accordingly.

description.description: Optional additional instructions or description to guide the pose generation.

n.description: Number of pose variations to generate (1-4).

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

url.description: URL of the generated pose image

pose.description: The pose that was used to generate this image

description.description: The pose description that was used to generate this image

motion_prompt.description: A suggested motion prompt optimized for this pose. Can be used directly with 'animateSprite' for best animation results.

TransferMotionPayload.description: Payload for transferring motion from a video onto a static sprite image, producing an animated spritesheet.

image.description: Payload for generating an animated spritesheet from a static image. Input images can either be provided in base64 or URL. If the image was generated using Ludo, ideally it should be generated using the "sprite", "sprite-vfx" or "ui_asset" type.

video.description: URL of the video to use as motion source. You can use videos from the animateSprite endpoint or provide your own. Videos up to 4 seconds will produce better results. Either `video` or `preset_id` + `perspective` + `direction` must be provided.

preset_id.description: ID of an animation preset to use instead of a video URL. Use the animation-presets endpoint to list available presets. When using a preset, `perspective` and `direction` are required.

direction.description: Direction for the animation preset. When using a preset, `direction` is required.

perspective.description: Perspective ID to use with the animation preset. When using a preset, `perspective` is required.

frames.description: Number of frames in the output spritesheet.

frame_size.description: Size of each frame in pixels (width and height). 0 is for maximum resolution.

loop.description: Trim the animation at the beginning or end to create a seamless loop.

crop.description: Crop sprite frames to fit content. Results in smaller spritesheets but inconsistent frame sizes across different animations.

margin_ratio.description: Amount of padding around the sprite as a ratio (0.0 to 1.0). Only used when margin_ratio_mode is "manual".

margin_ratio_mode.description: Controls how margins are applied around the sprite.

gif.description: When true, generates an animated GIF from the spritesheet and returns it in gif_url. Disabled by default to reduce response time.

individual_frames.description: When true, extracts each frame from the spritesheet as an individual image and returns the URLs in individual_frame_urls.

spritesheet_with_background.description: When true, also returns the spritesheet with background intact (before background removal). Useful for manually fixing background removal issues. The with-background spritesheet URL will be in spritesheet_with_background_url.

model.description:
Model to use. Available models:
- "tango" (Tango): 4 credits/s, min 4

duration.description:
Duration in seconds. If the reference video is longer, it will be compressed to this duration. Available values depend on the model:
- tango: 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5, 4

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateVideoPayloadPublic.description: Payload for generating a video from a source image and motion prompt

image.description: URL or base64-encoded source image (the starting frame for the video)

prompt.description: Text description of the motion or action for the video (e.g., "walking forward", "waving hand", "camera zoom in")

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

final_image.description: URL or base64-encoded end frame image. When provided, the video will interpolate between the initial and final frames.

model.description:
Model to use. Available models:
- "blitz" (Blitz): Visual Quality: 4/10 · Motion Stability: 8/10 · 1 credits/s
- "eagle" (Eagle): Visual Quality: 8/10 · Motion Stability: 5/10 · 1.3 credits/s
- "eagle-audio" (Eagle with Audio): Visual Quality: 8/10 · Motion Stability: 5/10 · 1.8 credits/s
- "chaos" (Chaos): Visual Quality: 6/10 · Motion Stability: 3/10 · 0.6 credits/s
Legacy aliases: "standard" → blitz, "new" → chaos.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

url.description: URL to the generated video file

duration.description: Duration of the video in seconds

RemoveBackgroundPayload.description: Payload for removing the background of an image

image.description: URL or base64-encoded image to remove the background from.

crop.description: Whether to crop/trim the result to fit the content.

creative_edit.description: Higher quality results but the image will not be exactly the same as the input.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

image.description: URL or base64-encoded image to convert to 3D

target_num_faces.description: Maximum triangle count for the mesh (1000-200000)

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

model_url.description: URL to the generated 3D model file

snapshots.description: URLs to snapshot images from different angles (useful for previews and MCP agents)

GenerateSoundEffectPayload.description: Payload for generating a sound effect from a text description

description.description: Text description of the sound effect to generate (e.g., "laser gun firing", "footsteps on gravel").

duration.description: Duration in seconds. Use 0 for automatic duration based on the description.

loop.description: Generate a sound effect that loops seamlessly.

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

url.description: URL to the generated audio file

GenerateAmbiancePayload.description: Payload for generating a seamless looping ambiance soundscape from a text description

description.description: Text description of the ambiance to generate (e.g., "windy forest at dusk", "busy tavern interior").

duration.description: Duration in seconds. Use 0 for automatic duration based on the description.

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateMusicPayload.description: Payload for generating music from a text description

description.description: Text description of the music to generate (e.g., "epic orchestral battle theme", "calm piano melody").

lyrics.description: Optional lyrics to include in the generated music.

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateVoicePayload.description: Payload for generating a voice sample from a character description

voice_description.description: Text description of the voice character (e.g., "deep-voiced warrior", "cheerful young girl").

text.description: Text for the voice to speak (max 20 words).

augment_prompt.description: Augment the prompt behind the scenes. Disable to have more control.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateSpeechPayload.description: Payload for text-to-speech generation using voice cloning

text.description: Text to convert to speech (max 100 words).

sample.description: URL or base64-encoded audio sample for voice cloning.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.

GenerateSpeechPresetPayload.description: Payload for text-to-speech generation using a voice preset

text.description: Text to convert to speech (max 100 words).

emotion.description: Emotion to apply to the speech.

request_id.description: Optional client-provided identifier for this request. Use this to retrieve results later via the results endpoint.
